summary: iter-205 backend testing of Alpha Bio implant catalog expansion. All 8 Alpha Bio systems verified with expected rich component counts (SPI/NeO Internal Hex/ICE/ATID/DFI = 50, NeO Conical Standard = 36, NeO Conical Hex / NICE = 31). Backend was restarted to confirm seed_if_thin idempotency; counts remained unchanged. All 24 pytest tests passed (100%).
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/implant-catalog response shape uses {'systems': [...]} wrapper and component objects use field name 'type' (not 'component_type'). Other places in the codebase may use 'component_type'; consistency check recommended for compare endpoint vs main catalog payload.

Backend startup hook calls _seed_implant_catalog() then seed_if_thin() — the gate based on len(components) < THRESHOLD looks correct and idempotent across restarts. Confirmed by restart-then-verify cycle.

There is an extra non-AlphaBio entry in catalog labelled 'Surgical & Prosthetic Instrumentation' with components=[] and is_stub=null — could be intentional grouping but worth confirming it isn't surfaced as a selectable implant system on the UI.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded catalog state. Verified that supervisorctl restart backend does NOT regress AlphaBio component counts (seed_if_thin is correctly idempotent).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Component-count spec for AlphaBio: SPI=50, NeO Conical Standard=36, NeO Conical Hex=31, NeO Internal Hex=50, ICE=50, ATID=50, DFI=50, NICE=31 (total 338). API: GET /api/implant-catalog -> {'systems': [...]}, items have brand='Alpha Bio', name=<systemName>, components=[{type, subtype, platform (CS/CHC/IH), material[], retention[], catalog_code, torque_ncm, indication, ...}]. by-key endpoint: /api/implant-catalog/by-key?key=Alpha%20Bio%7C<SystemName>. compare endpoint: /api/implant-catalog/compare?component_type=<type> returns {'component_type','systems','total_systems'}. Pytest entry point: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_alpha_bio_catalog.py (24 cases, all passing).
rca of the issue: N/A — feature delivery verification, no defect found. Initial test failure was due to test stub not knowing API response wrapped items under 'systems'; corrected, then 24/24 passed before AND after a fresh backend restart.
